name: Coltrane Chimurenga
description: Black liberation organizer, December 12th Movement field marshal, and the federal government's named 'ringleader' of the New York 8+ federal RICO prosecution — a 1984-85 case widely understood as a follow-on to the post-Brink's wave of Black liberation prosecutions. Arrested in a predawn raid on October 17-18, 1984 by roughly 500 FBI/NYPD Joint Terrorism Task Force agents and charged with conspiring to break Sundiata Acoli out of federal prison, carry out armed bank expropriations, and bomb federal buildings. Held without bail in federal custody under the just-enacted 1984 Bail Reform Act until Judge David N. Edelstein granted him release on $500,000 bond — about three and a half months in MCC New York. The Second Circuit affirmed his release in April 1985 (United States v. Chimurenga, 760 F.2d 400). Trial began May 13, 1985 in the Southern District of New York. The August 1985 jury acquitted Chimurenga and his co-defendants of every RICO and conspiracy count and convicted him only on minor weapons-possession and false-identification counts to which the defense had not contested. He was sentenced in mid-January 1986. After release he became a longtime field marshal of the December 12th Movement and a leading New York City organizer until his death on May 7, 2019.

cases.charges: RICO conspiracy; conspiracy to commit armed bank robberies, bombings of federal buildings, and prison escape (Sundiata Acoli); possession of firearms and false-identification documents (US v. Chimurenga, 760 F.2d 400)
cases.convicted: Acquitted of all RICO / conspiracy counts (Aug 1985); convicted only of uncontested weapons-possession and false-ID counts
cases.judge: Kevin Thomas Duffy (trial); David N. Edelstein (bail / preventive detention)

name: Omowale Clay
description: Black liberation organizer and longtime co-founder of the December 12th Movement, charged as a New York 8+ defendant in the October 17-18, 1984 federal RICO indictment alleging a Black liberation conspiracy to free Sundiata Acoli from prison, commit armed bank expropriations, and bomb federal buildings. Arrested in a predawn FBI/NYPD Joint Terrorism Task Force raid. Held without bail in federal custody under the 1984 Bail Reform Act for roughly three to four months before being released pending trial in early 1985. Trial began May 13, 1985 before Judge Kevin Thomas Duffy in the Southern District of New York; on August 5, 1985 the jury acquitted him and his co-defendants of all RICO and conspiracy counts. After his release he continued organizing through the December 12th Movement.

cases.charges: RICO conspiracy; conspiracy to commit armed bank robberies, bombings, and prison escape
cases.convicted: Acquitted of all RICO / conspiracy counts (Aug 1985)

name: Viola Plummer
description: Black liberation organizer and co-founder of the December 12th Movement, charged as a New York 8+ defendant in the October 17-18, 1984 federal RICO indictment alleging a conspiracy to free Sundiata Acoli, commit armed bank expropriations, and bomb federal buildings. Arrested in a predawn FBI/NYPD Joint Terrorism Task Force raid and held without bail under the 1984 Bail Reform Act before being released pending trial in early 1985. Trial began May 13, 1985 before Judge Kevin Thomas Duffy in the Southern District of New York; in August 1985 she was acquitted of every RICO and conspiracy count, convicted only of one minor count of falsely identifying herself to gain admittance to the MCC. After release, she became one of New York City's most prominent Black liberation organizers, co-chair of the December 12th Movement, and a leader of the international human-rights coalition WBAI Pacifica defenders; she also served as chief of staff to New York City Councilman Charles Barron until her death on March 11, 2024.

cases.charges: RICO conspiracy; conspiracy to commit armed bank robberies, bombings, and prison escape; one count of false identification to gain admittance to MCC
cases.convicted: Acquitted of all RICO / conspiracy counts (Aug 1985); convicted of one minor false-ID count

name: Roger Wareham
description: Civil rights attorney and December 12th Movement co-founder, charged as a New York 8+ defendant in the October 17-18, 1984 federal RICO indictment alleging a Black liberation conspiracy to free Sundiata Acoli, commit armed bank expropriations, and bomb federal buildings. Arrested in a predawn FBI/NYPD Joint Terrorism Task Force raid and held without bail in federal custody before being released pending trial in early 1985. Trial began May 13, 1985 before Judge Kevin Thomas Duffy in the Southern District of New York; the August 1985 jury acquitted him on every count. He continued practicing reparations law and pan-African human-rights advocacy, serving as legal counsel to the International Association Against Torture and to delegations on the rights of African people before the United Nations Working Group of Experts on People of African Descent.

cases.charges: RICO conspiracy; conspiracy to commit armed bank robberies, bombings, and prison escape

Trial began May 13, 1985 before Judge Kevin Thomas Duffy in the Southern District of New York; the August 1985 jury acquitted her of all conspiracy counts.

name: Jose Rios
description: Black / Latino liberation activist charged in the October 17-18, 1984 federal RICO indictment alongside the New York 8 — making the case the New York 8+. The sole charge against him was RICO conspiracy. Arrested in a predawn FBI/NYPD Joint Terrorism Task Force raid and held without bail in federal custody before being released pending trial in early 1985. Trial began May 13, 1985 before Judge Kevin Thomas Duffy in the Southern District of New York; the August 1985 jury acquitted him on the only count he faced.
